blocks:
Sūrat Nūḥ (Noah) (peace be upon him)

(Meccan, its number of verses is twenty-eight verses) بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً إِلَى قَوْمِهِ أَنْ أَنْذِرْ قَوْمَكَ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. (1) We certainly sent Noah to his people, [saying,] 'Warn your people before a painful punishment overtakes them.' (٢) قالَ يا قَوْمِ إِنِّي لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ. (2) He said, 'O my people! I am indeed a manifest warner to you, (٣) أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَاتَّقُوهُ وَأَطِيعُونِ. (3) that you shall worship Allah, be wary of Him, and obey me. (٤) يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ مِنْ ذُنُوبِكُمْ ** (4) He will forgive you some of your sins It was said: some of your sins, and that is what preceded, for indeed, Islam wipes it out (يَجُبُّهُ). وَيُؤَخِّرْكُمْ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** It is the maximum limit that He has decreed for you, subject to the conditions of faith (الإِيمان) and obedience (الطاعة). إِنَّ أَجَلَ اللهِ ** Indeed, the term (الأجل) that Allah has decreed, when it comes, it will not be deferred. So hasten during the times of respite (الإمهال) and deferment (التأخير). لَوْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ ** comes, it will not be deferred, if you knew.' (If you knew) the truth of that and believed in it, (meaning) that because of their immersion (انهماكهم) in the love of life, it is as if they are doubting death because of it.

(٥) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي دَعَوْتُ قَوْمِي لَيْلاً وَنَهاراً ** (5) He said, 'My Lord! I have indeed invited my people night and day, meaning, constantly. (٦) فَلَمْ يَزِدْهُمْ دُعائِي إِلَّا فِراراً ** (6) but my invitation has only increased their flight [from the truth]. from faith and obedience. (٧) وَإِنِّي كُلَّما دَعَوْتُهُمْ ** (7) And indeed, every time I invited them, that You might forgive them, they would put their fingers in their ears, cover themselves with their garments, persist [in their error], and display excessive arrogance. to faith ** لِتَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ ** because of it ** جَعَلُوا أَصابِعَهُمْ فِي آذانِهِمْ ** they blocked their hearing from listening to the truth of the call وَاسْتَغْشَوْا ثِيابَهُمْ Al-Qummi said: They concealed themselves (استتروا). ** وَأَصَرُّوا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا اسْتِكْباراً ** He said: They resolved not to hear anything.

(٨) ثُمَّ إِنِّي دَعَوْتُهُمْ جِهاراً. (8) Then I called them openly. (٩) ثُمَّ إِنِّي أَعْلَنْتُ لَهُمْ وَأَسْرَرْتُ لَهُمْ إِسْراراً ** (9) Then I addressed them publicly and spoke to them secretly in private. meaning, I called them time after time, and repetition after the first instance, secretly and openly, and in every manner possible for me. And the word ثُمَّ (then) is used due to the difference (تفاوت) in the methods or because some methods followed others sequentially (لتراخي بعضها عن بعض). (١٠) فَقُلْتُ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ** (10) I said, ‘Plead with your Lord for forgiveness. by repenting from disobedience. إِنَّهُ كانَ غَفَّاراً ** (Indeed, He is all-forgiving) to those who repent. (١١) يُرْسِلِ السَّماءَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِدْراراً ** (11) Indeed, He is all-forgiving. He will send down for you the sky pouring down abundance, abundant in yield (كثير الدرّ). (١٢) وَيُمْدِدْكُمْ بِأَمْوالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ ** (12) and aid you with wealth and sons, and appoint for you gardens gardens (بساتين). وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ أَنْهاراً ** (and appoint for you rivers). It is said that when his call became prolonged and their persistence (إصرارهم) continued, Allah withheld the rain (القطر) from them for forty years and made the wombs of their women barren (أعقم أرحام نسائهم). So, He promised them that [reward]. Their story has already preceded in Surat Hud (peace be upon him).

(13) ما لَكُمْ لا تَرْجُونَ لِلَّهِ وَقاراً (What is the matter with you that you do not expect any majesty from Allah) Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, said: "You do not fear Allah's greatness." (14) وَقَدْ خَلَقَكُمْ أَطْواراً ** (while He has created you in various stages?) Al-Qummi said: [It means] according to the difference in desires (الاهواء), intentions (الإرادات), and wills (المشيئات). And it was said: meaning stages (تارات): dust (تراباً), then a drop of sperm (نطفة), then a clinging mass (علقة), then a chewed lump of flesh (مضغة), then bones (عظاماً) and flesh (لحوماً), then He brought him forth as another creation (خلقاً آخر). For indeed, this indicates the greatness of His power and the perfection of His wisdom.

(١٥) أَلَمْ تَرَوْا كَيْفَ خَلَقَ اللهُ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ طِباقاً ** (15) Do you not see how Allah has created seven heavens in layers, some above others. (١٦) وَجَعَلَ الْقَمَرَ فِيهِنَّ نُوراً وَجَعَلَ الشَّمْسَ سِراجاً ** (16) and set the moon in them as a light, and set the sun as a lamp? He likened it (the sun) to it (the lamp) because it removes the darkness of the night from the face of the earth, just as the lamp removes it from what is around it. (١٧) وَاللهُ أَنْبَتَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ نَباتاً ** (17) Allah has made you grow out of the earth like a growth. He originated you from it. (١٨) ثُمَّ يُعِيدُكُمْ فِيها ** (18) Then He will bring you back into it, buried, وَيُخْرِجُكُمْ إِخْراجاً ** and bring you forth in an [ultimate] bringing forth. by means of the Gathering (بالحشر). (١٩) وَاللهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ بِساطاً ** (19) Allah has made the earth a carpet for you, upon which you move about. (٢٠) لِتَسْلُكُوا مِنْها سُبُلاً فِجاجاً ** (20) so that you may travel in its broad ways. wide (واسعة), which is the plural of (فج). The meaning of traversing (السلوك) implies the meaning of taking (الاتخاذ), so it was made transitive using (من). (٢١) قالَ نُوحٌ رَبِّ إِنَّهُمْ عَصَوْنِي ** (21) Noah said, 'My Lord! They have defied me, regarding what I commanded them with, وَاتَّبَعُوا مَنْ لَمْ يَزِدْهُ مالُهُ وَوَلَدُهُ and followed him whose wealth and children did not increase him إِلَّا خَساراً ** but only in loss. And they followed their leaders (رؤساءهم), who were insolent (البطرين) due to their wealth and deluded (المغترين) by their children, such that this became a cause for the increase of their loss in the Hereafter. And in this (Ayah) is the indication that they only followed them due to the prestige (وجاهة) they acquired through wealth and children, which led them to loss (الخسار). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: "And they followed the wealthy (الأغنياء)." And the word (وولده) is read with ḍammah (الضمّ) and sukūn (السّكون).

وَمَكَرُوا مَكْراً كُبَّاراً (٢٢) (and they devised a colossal plot.) exceedingly great. وَقالُوا لا تَذَرُنَّ آلِهَتَكُمْ (٢٣) (and said, 'Do not abandon your gods,) meaning, the worship of them. وَلا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّا وَلا سُواعاً وَلا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْراً ** (nor abandon Wadd, nor Suwa‘, nor Yaghuth, nor Ya‘uq, nor Nasr.') And do not abandon these specifically. It is said that these are the names of righteous men who lived between Adam and Noah. When they died, their images were made for the sake of seeking blessing (تبرّكاً) through them. When time passed, they were worshipped. And they were transferred to the Arabs. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: There was a group of believers before Noah, and they died, so the people grieved over them. Then Iblis came and made images of them so that they might feel comforted by them, and they became comforted by them. When winter came to them, they brought them into the houses. That generation passed, and the next generation came. Then Iblis came to them and said to them that these were gods whom their fathers used to worship, so they worshipped them, and a great many humans went astray because of them. So Noah (peace be upon him) prayed against them, and Allah destroyed them. And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that which is close to it [the previous narration]. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Wadd (ودّ) was an idol for Kalb, Suwa‘ (سواع) for Hudhayl, Yaghuth (يغوث) for Murad, Ya‘uq (يعوق) for Hamdan, and Nasr (نسر) for Husayn. And Wadd (ودّاً) was read with a dammah (بالضمّ).

(٢٤) وَقَدْ أَضَلُّوا كَثِيراً ** (24) and they have certainly led many astray. Meaning the chiefs (الرؤساء) or the idols (الأصنام). وَلا تَزِدِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا ضَلالاً ** (And do not increase the wrongdoers except in error.) Al-Qummi said: destruction (هلاكاً) and ruin (تدميراً).

(٢٥) مِمَّا خَطِيئاتِهِمْ ** (25) Because of their sins Due to their sins (خطيئاتهم). The مَا (mā) is extra (مزيدة) for emphasis (للتأكيد) and magnification (التفخيم). And it was recited (وقرئ): ممّا خطاياهم. أُغْرِقُوا ** (They were drowned) by the flood (بالطوفان). فَأُدْخِلُوا نَاراً فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَنْصَاراً ** (Then they were admitted into a Fire, and they found no helpers for themselves besides Allah) since their deities (آلهتهم) were unable to help them (نَصرهم).

(٢٦) وَقالَ نُوحٌ رَبِّ لا تَذَرْ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ دَيَّاراً ** (26) Noah said, 'My Lord! Do not leave any inhabitant of the faithless on the earth.) Meaning: anyone (أحداً). (٢٧) إِنَّكَ إِنْ تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا عِبادَكَ وَلا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فاجِراً كَفَّاراً. (27) For if You leave them, they will lead Your servants astray, and will not be beget except vicious ingrates.) Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked what the knowledge of Noah was when he supplicated against his people (knowing) that they لا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فاجِراً كَفَّاراً ** (will not beget except vicious ingrates). He (Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام) said: Have you not heard the saying of Allah the Almighty to Noah عليه السلام: أَنَّهُ لَنْ يُؤْمِنَ مِنْ قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَنْ قَدْ آمَنَ. (that none of your people will believe except those who have already believed.)

(٢٨) رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوالِدَيَّ وَلِمَنْ دَخَلَ بَيْتِيَ مُؤْمِناً. (28) 'My Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and whoever enters my house as a believer.' In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means the (الولاية). Whoever enters into the (الولاية) has entered the house of the Prophets. ** وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ وَلا تَزِدِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا تَباراً. 'and the believing men and women, and do not increase the wrongdoers except in ruin.' Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning (خساراً). In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever believes in Allah and reads His Book should not abandon the recitation of the Surah: إنّا أرسلنا نوحاً إلى قومه (Indeed, We sent Noah to his people). So, whichever servant recites it, seeking reward (محتسباً) and patiently (صابراً), whether in an obligatory prayer (فريضة) or a supererogatory prayer (نافلة), Allah will settle him in the dwellings of the righteous (الأبرار), and grant him three Gardens in addition to his Garden, as a bounty (كرامة) from Allah, and He will marry him to two hundred houris (حوراء) and four thousand houses, if Allah the Almighty wills.
